personas.label: CFO / Treasurer
personas.description: Capital allocation, financing, business case, financial reporting

personas.label: Tax director
personas.description: Credits, incentives, structuring, transfer pricing, compliance

personas.label: Chief development officer
personas.description: Site selection, JV structuring, due diligence, M&A

personas.label: Chief Legal / Commercial Officer
personas.description: Contracting strategy, risk allocation, commercial terms, power procurement, documentation rights

phases.label: Strategy
phases.tooltip: Defining the investment thesis, evaluating market opportunities, screening sites, and building the business case. This is where capital allocation decisions are made and the project's strategic rationale is established.

phases.label: Finance
phases.tooltip: Securing capital, structuring the deal, completing due diligence, and closing financing. Entity structuring, tax planning, and incentive capture strategies are locked in during this phase.

phases.label: Plan
phases.tooltip: Standing up governance, mobilizing the project team, finalizing design, awarding contracts, and establishing the controls framework. Procurement strategy and regulatory approvals are advanced in parallel.

phases.label: Build
phases.tooltip: Executing construction, managing contractors, controlling cost and schedule, and maintaining quality and safety standards. This is the highest-spend, highest-risk phase of the asset lifecycle.

phases.label: Operate
phases.tooltip: Running the asset at design capacity, managing energy and maintenance, ensuring compliance, and optimizing performance against benchmarks. Ongoing tax compliance and credit substantiation happen here.

phases.label: Dispose
phases.tooltip: Decommissioning, environmental remediation, asset sale or repurposing, and capturing lessons learned. Includes managing end-of-life contractual obligations and regulatory closeout.

categories.title: Capital & investment strategy
categories.risk: Committing capital to the wrong project, at the wrong scale, at the wrong time - and not finding out until you’re too far in to walk away.
lq: Are we building for the demand profile we’ll face in 5 years, or the one we see today - and what happens if the market moves faster than our construction timeline?
Have we pressure-tested whether our capital program can survive a significant shift in power costs, interest rates, or supply chain lead times hitting simultaneously?

items.name: Capital strategy & portfolio planning
q: Do we have a clear view of our total capital portfolio and where each asset sits in its lifecycle?
Are we allocating capital to the highest-return opportunities or just funding the loudest voices?
items.desc: Strategic planning and prioritization of capital investments across the asset portfolio, including lifecycle positioning, capital allocation frameworks, and long-range investment roadmaps.

items.name: Business case & feasibility
q: Are our assumptions stress-tested - what happens if costs overrun 20% or demand shifts?
Who owns the business case after approval, and how often do we revisit it against actuals?
items.desc: Development and stress-testing of investment business cases, including demand analysis, cost modeling, risk-adjusted returns, and ongoing tracking of actuals against approved assumptions.

items.name: Site assessment & location advisory
q: Are we evaluating sites against the full cost stack - land, power, labor, tax, permitting, resilience?
How does our site strategy align with our long-term energy and sustainability commitments?
items.desc: Evaluation of potential sites against the full cost stack — land, power availability, labor markets, tax and incentive landscape, permitting complexity, and long-term resilience factors.

items.name: Investment prioritization
q: When we have more opportunities than capital, what’s the decision framework?
Are we comparing projects on a consistent, risk-adjusted basis across the portfolio?
items.desc: Structured frameworks for ranking and selecting capital investments when opportunities exceed available capital, using consistent risk-adjusted criteria across the portfolio.

items.name: Post investment review
q: After major investments, are we systematically capturing what worked and what didn’t?
Are lessons from past projects actually feeding into the next business case?
items.desc: Systematic assessment of completed investments to capture lessons learned, compare outcomes to original business case assumptions, and feed insights into future decision-making.

items.name: Climate & resilience risk screening
q: Have we assessed the physical and transition climate risks to this asset over its expected life?
Could climate-related regulatory changes, energy cost shifts, or physical hazards fundamentally alter the investment thesis?
items.desc: Pre-investment assessment of physical and transition climate risks, including regulatory exposure, energy cost volatility, and long-term asset viability under multiple climate scenarios.

categories.title: Structuring, tax & incentives
categories.risk: Structuring in a way that leaves millions in uncaptured incentives, or structures that unravel under regulatory change or audit.
lq: If the incentive landscape shifts mid-project - credits reduced, clawback provisions triggered, or new compliance requirements - does our structure still work?
Are we confident that our tax and incentive strategy is being designed at the same pace as our engineering and procurement decisions, or is tax catching up after commitments are already made?

items.name: Tax credits & incentives
q: Are we identifying every eligible credit at federal, state, and local levels before breaking ground?
Are we structured to actually capture and monetize the credits we’re eligible for?
items.desc: Identification, structuring, and monetization of federal, state, and local tax credits — including ITC, PTC, and transferability provisions — to maximize after-tax project returns.

items.name: Grants, abatements & exemptions
q: Have we modeled the full incentive value over the asset’s life, including clawback risk?
Are we negotiating the best possible terms, or accepting standard packages?
items.desc: Evaluation, negotiation, and lifecycle management of government grants, property tax abatements, and sales tax exemptions, including clawback risk modeling and compliance monitoring.

items.name: Joint ventures & partnerships
q: Is our JV structure optimized for each partner’s tax position and risk appetite?
Who bears the downside if the partnership underperforms, and is that fair?
items.desc: Structuring and optimization of joint venture arrangements, including partner alignment on economics, tax positions, governance, risk allocation, and downside protection.

items.name: Entity & investor structuring
q: Is our entity structure fit for purpose across all jurisdictions, or carrying legacy complexity?
Can our current structure flex to accommodate different investor types without restructuring?
items.desc: Design of legal entity structures across jurisdictions to optimize tax efficiency, accommodate diverse investor types, and maintain flexibility for future capital events.

items.name: Tax optimization & transfer pricing
q: Are our intercompany arrangements defensible under audit?
Are we proactively managing transfer pricing as the business evolves, or waiting for a challenge?
items.desc: Ongoing management of intercompany arrangements and transfer pricing policies to ensure tax efficiency across the enterprise while maintaining audit defensibility.

items.name: Cost segregation analysis
q: Have we done a cost segregation study on recent builds to accelerate depreciation?
Are we leaving cash flow on the table by using default asset classifications?
items.desc: Engineering-based study of constructed or acquired assets to reclassify building components into shorter depreciation categories, accelerating tax deductions and improving cash flow.

items.name: Customs & trade planning
q: Are we factoring tariff exposure and trade policy volatility into procurement decisions?
Have we evaluated foreign trade zone benefits for our key sites?
items.desc: Strategic management of tariff exposure, trade policy volatility, and foreign trade zone benefits as they apply to equipment procurement, construction materials, and ongoing operations.

categories.title: Financing & transactions
categories.risk: Not being able to close financing, or closing on terms that erode returns - whether because capital markets shifted, diligence uncovered issues the model didn’t anticipate, or the project timeline slipped past the financing window.
lq: If our financing timeline slips by 6 months due to market volatility or permitting delays, do we have a capital bridge - or does the project stall?

Are we underwriting to a realistic base case on cost, schedule, and technical performance - or are we relying on assumptions that haven’t been independently stress-tested against what actually happens on projects of this scale?

items.name: Equity raise & lead advisory
q: Are we accessing the broadest set of capital sources with the right equity story?
Is our capital structure optimized for this phase of growth, or are we over- or under-equitized?
items.desc: Advisory on equity capital raises, including investor targeting, equity story development, capital structure optimization, and negotiation of terms with equity partners.

items.name: Debt raise & capital markets
q: Are our financing terms competitive, and have we stress-tested debt capacity under downside scenarios?
Do we have covenant headroom for the next 2–3 years under realistic assumptions?
items.desc: Structuring and execution of debt financing, including bank lending, bond issuances, and project finance — with stress-testing of covenant headroom and refinancing risk.

items.name: Financial due diligence
q: Do we have full visibility into the financial health of what we’re acquiring?
Are the seller’s projections grounded in contractual reality, or built on optimistic assumptions?
items.desc: Assessment of a target's financial health during a transaction, including quality of earnings, working capital analysis, debt and liability review, and validation of management projections.

items.name: Tax due diligence
q: Are there historical tax positions in the target that could create exposure post-close?
Are we capturing the full tax synergies available in this deal structure?
items.desc: Analysis of historical and current tax positions in an acquisition target to identify exposures, quantify risks, and capture available tax synergies in the deal structure.

items.name: Commercial due diligence
q: Is the market thesis behind this investment sound and independently validated?
Are demand projections based on contracts, letters of intent, or just management forecasts?
items.desc: Independent validation of the market thesis behind an investment, including demand drivers, competitive positioning, customer contract quality, and revenue sustainability.

items.name: Technical due diligence
q: What’s the true condition of the asset - are there deferred maintenance or capacity constraints?
What’s the real CapEx needed to bring this asset to our operating standard?
items.desc: Independent assessment of an asset's physical condition, design adequacy, deferred maintenance, capacity constraints, and the true capital expenditure required to meet operating standards.

items.name: Valuation
q: Is our valuation methodology appropriate and defensible to stakeholders?
Are we paying a fair price, or are competitive dynamics inflating the bid?
items.desc: Application of defensible valuation methodologies — DCF, comparable transactions, and precedent analysis — to establish fair value for transactions, reporting, or strategic planning.

items.name: Market entry & structuring
q: Are we entering this market with the right vehicle and at the right time?
What’s our competitive advantage versus incumbents, and is it sustainable?
items.desc: Advisory on entering new markets or asset classes, including competitive landscape assessment, regulatory environment, optimal entry vehicle, and go-to-market structuring.

items.name: Post-deal integration
q: Do we have a 100-day integration plan that protects value from day one?
Who is accountable for realizing the synergies we underwrote in the business case?
items.desc: Planning and execution of post-close integration, including day-one readiness, synergy tracking, organizational alignment, and the transition from deal team to operational ownership.

categories.title: Governance, risk & compliance
categories.risk: A regulatory, permitting, or community opposition issue stops the project cold - not because of engineering, but because of process, politics, or oversight failure.
lq: What is the single regulatory or permitting dependency that, if delayed 12 months, would fundamentally change the project economics - and what’s our plan B?
Are we treating community engagement and political risk with the same rigor as technical risk, or assuming approvals will come because the economics make sense?

items.name: Governance & setup
q: Do we have the right governance structure to make decisions at the speed this program requires?
Are decision rights clear, or do things stall in committee?
items.desc: Design and implementation of program governance structures, including decision rights, reporting cadences, escalation protocols, and committee frameworks scaled to program complexity.

items.name: Regulatory strategy
q: Are we ahead of the regulatory curve, or purely reactive?
Which upcoming regulations could fundamentally change our project economics?
items.desc: Proactive identification and management of regulatory requirements that could impact project economics, including permitting pathways, environmental approvals, and emerging regulatory trends.

items.name: Regulatory & governance compliance
q: Are we confident we’d pass a regulatory audit today?
Where are the compliance blind spots we haven’t stress-tested?
items.desc: Ongoing monitoring and assurance of compliance with regulatory obligations, permit conditions, and internal governance standards across the project lifecycle.

items.name: Stakeholder management
q: Have we mapped all stakeholders with a strategy for each?
Where could opposition derail the project, and do we have a mitigation plan?
items.desc: Mapping and engagement of all project stakeholders — community groups, regulators, political bodies, and partners — with tailored strategies to build support and mitigate opposition risk.

items.name: Organizational design
q: Is our organization designed around the work that needs to happen, or around legacy structures?
Do we have the right spans of control and decision authority for execution speed?
items.desc: Design of organizational structures, roles, and decision authority to match the operational demands of the program, moving beyond legacy structures to execution-oriented models.

items.name: Change management
q: Are the people who need to change actually bought in, or just compliant on paper?
What’s our plan when resistance surfaces - do we have early warning indicators?
items.desc: Structured approach to driving adoption and behavior change across the organization, including stakeholder readiness assessment, resistance management, and sustained engagement.

items.name: Sustainability, ESG & carbon reporting
q: Can we substantiate our ESG commitments with data, or are they still aspirational?
Are we prepared for the reporting requirements coming in the next 2–3 years?
items.desc: Measurement, reporting, and assurance of ESG commitments and carbon impact, including alignment with emerging disclosure frameworks and stakeholder reporting expectations.

items.name: Workforce strategy & capability building
q: Can we actually staff this program with the talent we need, in the markets we need it?
Are we building long-term capability, or just filling seats to hit a milestone?
items.desc: Planning for the talent and skills required to deliver and operate the asset, including labor market assessment, capability gap analysis, training programs, and long-term workforce development.

items.name: Health & safety advisory
q: Are our safety systems designed for the specific risks of this project?
Where are we most exposed, and are our leading indicators actually leading?
items.desc: Design and assurance of health and safety management systems tailored to project-specific risks, including leading indicator programs, incident response, and regulatory compliance.

items.name: Risk management framework
q: Do we have a risk register that’s actually used for decisions, or is it a compliance artifact?
What’s the risk we’re not talking about - the one that could change everything?
items.desc: Development and operationalization of enterprise risk frameworks that actively inform decisions — moving beyond compliance artifacts to tools that surface emerging and correlated risks.

categories.title: Program delivery & controls
categories.risk: The project runs over budget, over schedule, or delivers an asset that doesn’t perform to spec - the classic capital project failure mode.
lq: If our lead contractor or a critical equipment supplier fails to deliver, what’s our recovery plan - and have we actually tested it, or is it theoretical?
Are we managing this program with the same intensity in month 18 as we did in month 1, or has governance fatigue set in while the hardest work is still ahead?

items.name: Program & project management
q: Is our PMO set up to deliver, or is it just a reporting layer?
Do project managers have the authority to make the calls they need to make?
items.desc: Establishment and operation of the program management office, including governance, reporting, resource management, and the authority structure needed to drive execution.

items.name: Cost management & controls
q: Do we know our true cost position right now - not last month’s?
Where are the biggest variances trending, and what’s the corrective action plan?
items.desc: Real-time tracking and control of project costs, including budget management, variance analysis, forecasting, earned value reporting, and corrective action protocols.

items.name: Schedule & risk management
q: Are we managing schedule proactively, or just reporting delays after they happen?
What’s on the critical path today, and what would it take to recover if it slips?
items.desc: Proactive management of the project schedule, including critical path analysis, risk-based scheduling, delay mitigation, and recovery planning when milestones slip.

items.name: Procurement strategy
q: Are we getting the best value from our supply chain, or locked into suboptimal contracts?
Are we single-sourced on anything critical, and what’s our contingency?
items.desc: Development of sourcing strategies that balance cost, risk, and schedule — including contract type selection, market engagement, and supplier qualification frameworks.

items.name: Commercial management
q: Are our contracts actually protecting us in the ways that matter?
When was the last time we assessed our claims exposure across the program?
items.desc: Management of contractual relationships across the program lifecycle, including contract strategy, claims and disputes, change order administration, risk allocation, and commercial close-out.

items.name: Supply chain execution
q: Do we have visibility into Tier 2 and Tier 3 suppliers?
What happens if a key supplier fails - do we have a tested backup plan?
items.desc: End-to-end management of the project supply chain, including supplier performance monitoring, logistics coordination, Tier 2/3 visibility, and contingency planning for supplier failure.

items.name: Construction oversight
q: Are we confident the work in the field matches the design intent and quality spec?
Who is independently verifying quality, and are they truly independent?
items.desc: Independent monitoring of construction quality, progress, and compliance with design intent — ensuring the work in the field meets specifications before issues compound.

items.name: Commissioning & handover
q: Do we have a clear, agreed definition of ‘done’ that operations has signed off on?
Is operations ready to take over on day one, or will there be a capability gap?
items.desc: Structured process for testing, validating, and transferring the completed asset from the construction team to operations, including definition of completion criteria and readiness verification.

items.name: Design management
q: Are design decisions being made with the full cost-of-ownership in mind?
Are we designing for constructability and operability, not just performance on paper?
items.desc: Oversight of the design process to ensure decisions are made with full cost-of-ownership, constructability, and operability in mind — not just technical performance on paper.

items.name: Technical advisory
q: Do we have the right independent technical perspective, or are we relying entirely on the contractor?
Are our technical advisors asking hard questions, or confirming what we want to hear?
items.desc: Independent technical perspective on engineering decisions, contractor performance, and technical risk — providing the project owner with assurance beyond what the contractor delivers.

items.name: Lender & investor technical advisory
q: Are our lenders and investors getting the independent assurance they need?
Is there a gap between what we’re reporting and what they need to see?
items.desc: Independent technical assurance provided to lenders and investors, including progress reporting, risk assessment, and verification that the project meets financing requirements.

items.name: Vendor selection
q: Are we evaluating vendors against a consistent set of criteria - technical capability, financial stability, track record, and cultural fit?
Is our vendor selection process fast enough to keep pace with the project timeline without compromising rigor?
items.desc: Structured evaluation and selection of vendors and contractors against consistent criteria — technical capability, financial stability, track record, and alignment with project requirements.

categories.title: Technology & data
categories.risk: Making a major technology bet that becomes obsolete before the asset is operational, or failing to integrate systems in a way that creates blind spots.
lq: Are we making technology choices today that lock us into a vendor or architecture that may not be the right answer by the time this asset is fully operational?
Do we have a single source of truth across engineering, procurement, construction, and finance - or are we running parallel systems that will diverge under pressure?

items.name: Digital program enablement
q: Are our digital tools helping people do their jobs better, or just adding reporting overhead?
Are we getting value from what we’ve already deployed before investing in the next platform?
items.desc: Selection, deployment, and integration of digital tools to support project execution and reporting — including project controls platforms, collaboration tools, and data dashboards.

items.name: Advanced analytics
q: Are we using data to predict problems, or just documenting them after the fact?
What decisions would we make differently if we had better data today?
items.desc: Application of data analytics, predictive modeling, and machine learning to project and operational data to drive earlier, better-informed decisions across the asset lifecycle.

items.name: BIM / digital twin
q: Is our digital twin maintained and used for operations, or did it stop being useful after construction?
What’s the ROI case for maintaining it through the asset lifecycle?
items.desc: Creation and maintenance of building information models and digital twins that support design coordination during construction and asset management through operations.

items.name: ERP integration
q: Can our systems talk to each other, or are people manually bridging gaps with spreadsheets?
Where is integration friction costing us the most time and accuracy?
items.desc: Integration of enterprise resource planning systems across project and operational functions, eliminating manual data bridging and ensuring a connected information environment.

items.name: Process automation & orchestration
q: Which high-volume, low-value processes should be automated first?
What’s the business case - labor savings, speed, error reduction, or all three?
items.desc: Identification and implementation of automation opportunities across high-volume, low-value processes to improve speed, accuracy, and cost efficiency.

items.name: Data governance & reporting
q: Do we trust our data enough to make decisions on it without manual verification?
If the board asked for a number today, how quickly and confidently could we produce it?
items.desc: Establishment of data standards, ownership, and quality controls that enable trusted, auditable reporting and confident decision-making across the organization.

categories.title: Operations & asset optimization
categories.risk: The asset underperforms from day one because operations wasn’t ready, or ongoing costs erode the returns that justified the investment.
lq: If the asset reaches mechanical completion on schedule, can we actually operate it at design capacity from day one - and what’s the revenue impact of every month of ramp-up delay?
Are our operating cost assumptions based on benchmarked reality, or did we inherit the developer’s pro forma and never pressure-test it against actual comparable facilities?

items.name: Operational readiness planning
q: Will operations be genuinely ready on day one, or are we going to scramble?
Have we tested our operational processes before go-live, not just documented them?
items.desc: Structured preparation to ensure operations teams, processes, systems, and supply chains are genuinely ready to take over and perform at design capacity from day one of commercial operation.

items.name: Asset management strategy
q: Are we managing assets for maximum lifecycle value, or just keeping them running?
When does it make more sense to replace than maintain - and do we have a framework for that call?
items.desc: Long-term strategy for maximizing asset value over its full lifecycle, including maintenance optimization, capital renewal planning, and replacement-versus-repair decision frameworks.

items.name: Energy management, efficiency & resilience
q: What’s our energy cost per unit of output versus peers?
Are we resilient to grid disruption or price spikes, or is that a blind spot?
items.desc: Optimization of energy procurement, consumption, and resilience across operating assets, including power contract strategy, demand management, grid risk mitigation, and peer benchmarking.

items.name: Financial reporting & technical accounting
q: Are our reporting processes efficient, or are we throwing people at close cycles?
Can we report with both confidence and speed when stakeholders need answers?
items.desc: Efficient and accurate financial reporting processes, including technical accounting for complex asset structures, close-cycle optimization, and stakeholder-ready reporting.

items.name: Internal cost audit & benchmarking
q: Are we benchmarking against the right peers with the right metrics?
Where do our operations underperform, and do we understand the root cause?
items.desc: Systematic review of operating costs against peer benchmarks and internal targets, identifying underperformance root causes and opportunities for efficiency improvement.

items.name: Credit substantiation & audit readiness
q: Can we defend every credit we’ve claimed with documentation?
If audited tomorrow, what would we be scrambling to pull together?
items.desc: Documentation, tracking, and defense of all claimed tax credits, ensuring complete audit trails and readiness for federal, state, or local examination at any time.

items.name: Customs & trade compliance
q: Are we managing duty exposure proactively, or finding out about costs at the border?
Are our foreign trade zone benefits fully realized?
items.desc: Ongoing management of duty exposure, import/export compliance, and foreign trade zone benefits across operating assets and supply chains.

items.name: Climate risk modeling
q: Are we actively monitoring and adapting to physical climate risks - extreme weather, water stress, grid reliability - at each operating site?
Are our climate risk disclosures and reporting keeping pace with evolving regulatory and stakeholder expectations?
items.desc: Active monitoring and adaptation to physical climate risks at operating sites, including extreme weather exposure, water stress, grid reliability, and alignment with evolving disclosure requirements.

items.name: Cybersecurity, data privacy & crisis response
q: If we were breached today, what’s our response plan and who leads it?
Are our operational technology systems as protected as our IT systems?
items.desc: Protection of operational technology and IT systems from cyber threats, management of data privacy obligations, and maintenance of tested incident response and crisis management plans.

items.name: Facility operate, maintain & retire
q: Is our maintenance strategy predictive or reactive?
Are we spending the right amount on maintenance - not too much, not too little?
items.desc: Day-to-day operation and maintenance of the physical asset, including predictive maintenance strategies, condition monitoring, and lifecycle cost optimization.

items.name: Decommissioning & disposal
q: Do we know our decommissioning obligations, and are they funded?
Are there environmental liabilities we haven’t quantified or disclosed?
items.desc: Planning and execution of end-of-life activities, including environmental remediation, regulatory closeout, asset disposal or repurposing, and management of decommissioning obligations.

items.name: Operational turnaround & restructuring
q: If an asset is underperforming, do we have a playbook to diagnose and fix it fast?
At what point do we cut losses versus invest in a turnaround - and who makes that call?
items.desc: Rapid diagnosis and remediation of underperforming assets, including root cause analysis, operational restructuring, performance recovery planning, and go/no-go decision frameworks.
